_meta.title: Per-variant Greg gompit mortality tier policy (MORTDRIVER)
_meta.description: Source of truth mapping each confirmed FVS-modern variant to the recommended Greg gompit mortality coefficient tier. Decided from a per-variant 4-arm A/B on held-out FIA remeasurement pairs (native FVS vs crown-only gompit vs size-extended gompit vs size+BGI gompit; log-loss, AUC, bias). Unlike DGDRIVER, this is NOT a swappable site-driver family: the merged GOMPSURV form is an additive TIER structure, crown-only (5 coef) -> +size (6 coef, adds log(DBH)) -> +size+BGI (7 coef, adds BGI on top of size). The MORTDRVR keyword is a reproducibility-logging device only for now; actual tier selection happens by which coefficient file is passed to FVS_GOMPIT_COEF.

codes.1: size-extended gompit, available but not recommended standalone (config/greg_mortality_coefficients_size.csv)

_meta.basis: in-engine per-variant 4-arm A/B on held-out FIA remeasurement pairs (native FVS, crown-only gompit, size-extended gompit, size+BGI gompit), 2026-07
_meta.verdict: size+BGI is a strict win on both log-loss and AUC in all six confirmed variants (ne, cs, ls, wc, ie, ak); the cleanest, most general result of this effort. Size-only is NOT reliably better than crown-only: it hurts AUC in ie (0.638 vs 0.654) and ak (0.615 vs 0.622), and hurts log-loss outright in ak (0.577 vs 0.561, worse than native). Policy: crown-only stays the engine's byte-identical default (unset keyword / MORTDRIVER code 0); size+BGI (code 2) is the recommended opt-in upgrade for all six confirmed variants; size-only (code 1) is retained as an available/diagnostic tier but is not the recommended choice anywhere. ak carries a lower-confidence flag due to a thin sample (33 plots) but size+BGI still wins there so it is included in the recommended set, annotated as lower confidence.
_meta.backward_compatible: Emitting MORTDRVR is opt-in. A generate_keywords()-style call or the mortdriver_keyword() helper must be called explicitly; default engine behavior (no keyword, crown-only gompit) and the v2026.07-calibrated tag are unaffected.
_meta.unscreened_note: Other CONUS variants are unscreened for mortality tiers (no 4-arm A/B run yet). Extending this screen to the remaining variants is a natural next step and is out of scope for this pass.

ak.note: thin sample: 33 plots, 13/92 species matched; size-only tier is worse than crown-only and native on log-loss, but size+BGI still wins here, so included in the recommended set with a low-confidence flag
